Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hartog appeared by telephone for Bank of The West (Trustee). Joseph Doherty for Tony La Russa's Animal Rescue Foundation, The Humane Society and the SPCA.

Mr. Doherty stated that he had nothing to add. Mr. Hartog provided history and explained that approval of this accounting with no objections was the last stage of the settlement agreement and that the petition is in a position to be granted. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Continued To Jan-14-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James O'Brien appeared by telephone for Bank of The West. Amanda Breck appeared by telephone for Humane Society.

Mr. O'Brien stated that they resolved the one sentence issue, that the Parties have agreed to a settlement agreement and that they are going to submit/file a Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement within three weeks. Ms. Breck requested a one week deadline to have the petition filed. The accounting petition was continued to January 14, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing of the petition for approval of settlement agreement (petition to be filed by January 10, 2014).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Continued To Dec-17-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James O'Brien for Bank of The West (Trustee). Brian Mulry for Tony La Russa's Animal Rescue Foundation, The Humane Society of The United States and the Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals.

Counsel requested a continuance stating that they are down to one sentence with regard to clarification that the funds will go to the Perpetual Beneficiaries and that they might go back to mediation. Mr. O'Brien also stated that the plan is to come back for Court approval of the settlement. The accounting petition was continued to December 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Continued To Nov-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tom Shaver for Bank of the West. Brian Mulry for Tony La Russa's Animal Rescue Foundation, The Humane Society of The United States and the Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals International.

The Court noted that Mr. Handelman was not present. Mr. Shaver stated that they were close to a settlement agreement and that they were arguing about specific terms of the mutual releases. Mr. Mulry requested a continuance to mid November. The accounting petition was continued to November 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of settlement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Continued To Oct-08-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Continued To Sep-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Sep-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hartag for Bank of The West. Albert Handelman for Thomas O'Toole (Special Trustee). Brian Mulry appeared in lieu of Gregory McCoy for Perpetual Beneficiaries.

The Parties are in dispute as to whether the Perpetual Beneficiaries have a right to remove the Trustee. Mr. Hartog reported that there are aproximately 20 properties, that the others have been sold and spoke of the various loans on the properties. Mr. Handelman stated that they don't care about who the Successor Trustee is and that their only concern is that the Special Trustee's actions be set accurately. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to talk about how to proceed. Upon returning, Counsel indicated that they agree on Alternative Dispute Resolution and asked to return to report to the Court. The accounting petition was continued to September 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to notify the Probate Examiner by September 3, 2013 if the matter is resolved or if they need a continuance.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Off Calendar Jul-09-2007 Continued To Oct-15-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Brian Connors appearing for petitioner Jack Pessin. Michael Ching appearing for Tom O'Toole, respondent. A stipulation has been file to remove the venue and jurisdiction from today's calendar. Mr. Connors and Mr. Hartog will work on mediation. Matter is continued to 10/15 for status.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Drocco.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trusts; To Determine Validity Of Last Will And Testatment; And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jun-04-2007 Continued To Jul-09-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Brian Connors appearing for petitioner. John Hartog appearing for Bank of West, trustee. Michael Ching appearing for Tom O'Toole. Mr. Connors still needs to serve the World Wildlife Fund. Discovery is pending. Mr. Hartog and Mr. Connors have agreed informally to Bank of West filing a petition for probate in Contra Costa County but Mr. O'Toole did not agree to venue change. Jurisdiction and venue issue addressed. Counsel are willing to file briefs with points & authorities by 7/2. Hearing is set on 7/9. Mr. Hartog will file a petition to file probate here with 15 days notice to be set fo hearing on 7/9. Matter is continued to 7/9.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR# 5208. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: M. Loos.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trusts; To Determine Validity Of Last Will And Testatment; And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Apr-16-2007 Continued To Jun-04-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Brian Connors appearing for Jack Pessin. John Hartog appearing for trustee Bank of The West. Michael Ching specially appearing for special trustee Thomas O"Toole. Ian Sweedler, Deputy Attorney General appearing for AG. The AG wants to make sure all the trust beneficiary are properly served. Court's tentative ruling is the petition for instructions is granted but it will be subject to court review for defense and use of trust funds. Mr. Hartog accepted the tentative ruling. Oral argument presented by Mr. Connors. With the trust being challenged, Court's tentative ruling stands. Order signed.
The need to have summons issued on Mr. Connors' petition addressed. Court stated it is not appropriate to have summons issued. There is no will or will contest before the court. Mr. Connors asked for waiver of personnal service on trustee and special trustee. Court is not waiving notice. Trustee and special trustee's appearance today do not constitute an appearance that will waive notice. Petition to determine valdity of purported trusts is continued to 6/4, after the hearing on motion to quash service on 5/17.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.
